title: assigning queue to unpacked array larger than 256 results in c++ error instead of verilation error
body:
This might be related to #5439 .  It took a little experimenting to narrow down, but here's the code example:

```verilog
module top;
    localparam SIZE = 256;

    initial begin
        byte unsigned word[SIZE];
        byte unsigned byte_queue[$];
        for(int i = 0; i < SIZE; i++) begin
            byte_queue.push_back(8'haa);
        end

        word = byte_queue;
        $display("word[255]: 'h%h", word[255]);
    end
endmodule
```
I didn't check every value of SIZE, but it seems that with a value of 256 or less you get a normal verilator error:
```
%Error: queue_assign_to_unpacked_array.sv:11:16: VARREF 'top.unnamedblk1.byte_queue' is not an unpacked array, but is in an unpacked array context
   11 |         word = byte_queue;
      |                ^~~~~~~~~~
```
and with a value of 257 or higher for SIZE, you get a C++ compile error:
```
In file included from Vqueue_assign_to_unpacked_array__ALL.cpp:8:
Vqueue_assign_to_unpacked_array___024root__0__Slow.cpp: In function ‘void Vqueue_assign_to_unpacked_array___024root___eval_initial__TOP(Vqueue_assign_to_unpacked_array___024root*)’:
Vqueue_assign_to_unpacked_array___024root__0__Slow.cpp:41:50: error: no match for ‘operator=’ (operand types are ‘VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 257>’ and ‘VlQueue<unsigned char>’)
   41 |     top__DOT__unnamedblk1__DOT__word = vlSelfRef.top__DOT__unnamedblk1__DOT__byte_queue;
      |                                                  ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```
The verilator error is much more helpful than the C++ error.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

verilator -j "$(nproc)" --binary 

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

Latest master: g996ae35a1

What OS and distribution are you using?

rocky linux 9

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

sure
